message:
feat(demo_showcase): add dashboard scene module

- dashboard_scene.rs: New dashboard scene implementation
- scenes.rs: Register dashboard scene
- demo_showcase.rs: Wire up dashboard scene routing

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2rxj","title":"demo_showcase: RichLogger integration","description":"Integrate RichLogger into demo_showcase and route logs through Live when active.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.813671250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:37:29.565527710Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:37:29.565466356Z","close_reason":"RichLogger integration complete: --log-level flag (off|error|warn|info|debug|trace), init_logger() initializes RichLogger with shared console, demo emits debug logs for scene transitions. 76 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1gou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:10.277694461Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:12.822369090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.825595399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Integration

- Initialize `RichLogger` early in `main` when enabled by config.
- Use it to emit realistic logs during pipeline simulation (build/test/deploy).
- Avoid `println!` during Live; use console/logs so Live render hook can manage cursor control.

## Acceptance criteria

- Logs appear styled by level and include useful metadata (time/level/path where appropriate).
- Logger does not break non-interactive mode (still prints readable lines).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sk1","title":"demo_showcase: automated test suite (unit + e2e + snapshots)","description":"Add comprehensive unit + integration + snapshot tests for demo_showcase (with detailed logging) to prevent regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.949498076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:00:32.249889601Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:00:32.249829930Z","close_reason":"Test suite implemented: demo_showcase_e2e.rs (39KB), demo_showcase_smoke.rs, demo_showcase_snapshots.rs, demo_showcase_harness.rs, plus 110 unit tests. Child beads bd-6tj5, bd-11zc, bd-3qbk, bd-zzss, bd-yzr8, bd-3p3h, bd-1e7c all closed. Remaining children (bd-61ky, bd-3czr) blocked by unfinished features.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.968252493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

The showcase is meant to be a *flagship* artifact, so it needs flagship testing:
- Output regressions are subtle and easy to introduce during refactors.
- Live/TTY behavior can break silently without automated checks.
- Export artifacts (HTML/SVG) are particularly prone to drift.

## Testing philosophy

We want three layers:

1. **Unit tests (fast, internal)**
   - CLI parsing, config defaults, scene registry ordering
   - small pure helpers (badges/formatting)

2. **Integration/e2e smoke tests (black-box)**
   - Spawn the compiled binary via `CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase`
   - Run each scene with CI-safe flags (`--no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick`)
   - Assert exit code, bounded runtime (timeout), and key output markers

3. **Snapshot/golden tests (visual regression)**
   - Capture deterministic text output (fixed `--width/--height`, `--color-system none` or ANSI-stripped)
   - Use `insta` snapshots for key scenes

## Logging requirements

All integration tests should emit structured debug logs (via `test-log` + `tracing`) including:
- command line executed
- env overrides
- runtime duration
- output sizes
- first/last N lines on failure (without spamming CI)

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test` passes quickly and runs unit + basic smoke tests.
- `cargo test --features full,tracing` runs the full snapshot suite (including content scenes).
- E2E tests fail with actionable logs when a scene hangs or output changes unexpectedly.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077869634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077815984Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).
- Layout adapts to narrow terminals: if width < threshold, collapse sidebar or switch to single-column mode.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2t54","title":"demo_showcase: narrow width verification","description":"Verify behavior at narrow widths (via --width) and ensure layout switches appropriately.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenFlame","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.223474670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:34.435983466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:43:34.435922202Z","close_reason":"Verified narrow width behavior (70, 60, 50, 40 cols). Added 4 E2E tests: test_narrow_width_70_completes, test_narrow_width_50_completes, test_narrow_width_all_scenes, test_narrow_width_minimum_no_panic. All scenes render without panic at narrow widths.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.412903908Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-abyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:59.903944763Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-2t54","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## How to verify

Run with fixed width, e.g. `--width 70` and ensure:
- narrow layout mode engages
- tables/panels don't wrap into unreadable mush

## Acceptance criteria

- Narrow output still looks intentional.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2wxz","title":"demo_showcase: controlled failure narrative","description":"Define the ‘failure’ moment in the narrative (what fails, why) and ensure it drives useful output (logs + traceback + inspect).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.641139331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:45:14.797081032Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:45:14.797019567Z","close_reason":"Implemented failure narrative types: FailureScenario, FailureEvent, StackFrame with 4 scenarios (DatabaseTimeout, ConfigValidation, HealthCheckFailure, ResourceExhaustion)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:07.789456633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.656509441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Purpose

The “failure moment” is what makes the demo feel real: it shows how rich_rust helps when things go wrong.

## Requirements

- The failure must be believable and related to earlier output (services/pipeline/config).
- It must justify showing: logs, inspect/pretty, and traceback.
- After demonstrating the failure, the demo should recover and show a final summary (unless `--fail-fast`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:44Z"}]}
